content.body: A transformation changes the position or size of a shape. The original shape is the object; the result is the image.
REFLECTION: A mirror image of the shape in a line of reflection. Each point and its image are equal distances from the mirror line.

Reflection in x-axis: (x, y) → (x, −y)
Reflection in y-axis: (x, y) → (−x, y)
Reflection in y=x: (x, y) → (y, x)
ROTATION: Turning a shape about a fixed point (centre of rotation) by a given angle and direction (clockwise or anticlockwise).

Rotation 90° anticlockwise about origin: (x,y) → (−y, x)
Rotation 180° about origin: (x,y) → (−x, −y)
Rotation 90° clockwise about origin: (x,y) → (y, −x)

content.title: Reflection Example
content.body:
Reflect point A(3, 2) in the y-axis.
Rule: (x,y) → (−x, y)
Image A' = (−3, 2)

content.title: Rotation Example
content.body:
Rotate B(4, 1) by 90° anticlockwise about origin.
Rule: (x,y) → (−y, x)
Image B' = (−1, 4)

questions.question: Reflection of point (5, −3) in the x-axis is:
questions.explanation: Reflection in x-axis: (x,y)→(x,−y). So (5,−3)→(5,3).

questions.question: Reflection in y-axis changes:
questions.explanation: y-axis reflection: (x,y)→(−x,y). Only x-coordinate changes sign.

questions.question: Point P(2, 5) reflected in y=x gives:
questions.explanation: Reflection in y=x swaps coordinates: (x,y)→(y,x). So (2,5)→(5,2).

questions.question: Rotation 180° about origin maps (3, 4) to:
90° anticlockwise rotation about origin maps (2, −3) to:
Which transformation produces a mirror image?
questions.explanation: Reflection creates a mirror image of the shape in a line.

questions.question: After a rotation, the shape:
options: Changes orientation and position but not size
questions.answer: Changes orientation and position but not size
questions.explanation: Rotation is an isometry — size and shape are preserved, but orientation changes.

questions.question: 90° clockwise rotation maps (1, 4) to:
The line of reflection of (3,5) and its image (3,−5) is:
questions.explanation: y-coordinate changed sign, x stayed same → reflected in x-axis.

questions.question: Rotation 270° anticlockwise is equivalent to:
questions.explanation: 270° anticlockwise = 360°−270° = 90° clockwise. Same result.

questions.question: Triangle ABC has vertices A(1,1), B(3,1), C(2,3). After reflection in x-axis, B' is:
Centre of rotation is:
options: A fixed point the shape rotates around
questions.answer: A fixed point the shape rotates around
questions.explanation: The centre of rotation stays fixed; all other points rotate around it.

questions.question: Point Q(−2, 4) reflected in y-axis gives:
questions.explanation: y-axis reflection: (x,y)→(−x,y). (−2,4)→(2,4)

questions.question: How many degrees is a full rotation?
questions.explanation: A complete turn is 360°.

questions.question: 180° rotation about origin maps (−3, 5) to:
Reflection and rotation both:
options: Change the size of the shape
questions.explanation: Both are isometries — length and area are unchanged.

questions.question: 90° anticlockwise maps (5, 0) to:
A shape and its reflection are:
questions.explanation: Reflected shapes are mirror images — same size but flipped.

questions.question: Which rule describes 90° clockwise rotation?
questions.explanation: 90° clockwise: (x,y)→(y,−x). Remember: clockwise flips sign of second.

questions.question: To describe a rotation fully you need:
questions.explanation: Three pieces of information: where (centre), how much (angle), which way (clockwise/anticlockwise).

content.body: TRANSLATION: Slides a shape a fixed distance in a fixed direction. Every point moves the same amount. Described by a column vector.
ENLARGEMENT: Changes the size of a shape by a scale factor from a centre of enlargement.

Image distance from centre = Scale factor × Object distance from centre
Scale factor k > 1: enlargement
Scale factor 0 < k < 1: reduction
Scale factor k < 0: enlargement on opposite side

content.title: Translation
content.body:
Translate point A(2, 3) by vector (4/−1).
A' = (2+4, 3+(−1)) = (6, 2)

content.title: Enlargement
content.body:
Enlarge point P(3,2) from centre O(1,0), scale factor 2.
Vector from O to P = (2,2).
New vector = 2×(2,2) = (4,4).
Image P' = O + (4,4) = (5,4).

questions.question: Translation vector (3/−2) moves point (1,4) to:
In a translation, all points move:
questions.explanation: Translation is a slide — every point shifts by exactly the same vector.

questions.question: Scale factor 3 enlargement makes a shape:
questions.explanation: Each length becomes 3 times longer. Area becomes 9 times (3²).

questions.question: Point A(2,1) translated by (−1/3) gives:
Enlargement with scale factor 0.5 produces:
questions.explanation: Scale factor between 0 and 1 makes the image smaller than the object.

questions.question: To describe a translation you need:
questions.explanation: Translation is fully described by a column vector showing horizontal and vertical shift.

questions.question: A triangle with area 6cm² is enlarged by scale factor 3. New area =
questions.explanation: Area scale factor = k² = 9. New area = 6 × 9 = 54cm²

questions.question: Centre of enlargement O(0,0), scale factor 2. Point P(3,4) maps to:
A translation preserves:
questions.explanation: Translation slides the shape — nothing changes except position.

questions.question: If P(5,3) maps to P'(2,7) under a translation, the vector is:
Negative scale factor in enlargement:
questions.explanation: Negative scale factor produces an image on the opposite side of the centre of enlargement.

questions.question: Scale factor ½ enlargement from O(0,0) maps (8,6) to:
Object length 4cm, image length 10cm. Scale factor =
questions.explanation: k = image÷object = 10÷4 = 2.5

questions.question: To describe an enlargement fully you need:
questions.explanation: Enlargement requires: where (centre of enlargement) and how much (scale factor).

questions.question: Which transformation changes the size of a shape?
questions.explanation: Only enlargement changes the size. Reflection, rotation and translation are isometries.

questions.question: Centre O(2,1), scale factor 3. Object point P(3,2). Image P' =
Scale factor −2 maps P(3,1) from origin to:
questions.explanation: −2×(3,1)=(−6,−2). Negative scale factor inverts position.

questions.question: A photo is enlarged from 5cm to 20cm wide. Scale factor =
Four transformations studied are:
questions.explanation: These are the four standard geometric transformations in O-level Maths.
